rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motoin And Motoin To Deem Facts Admitted And Strike The Answers Of Defendants Jill Kinney John Kinney And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 27, 2013, Line 1 - PLAINTIFF FRANK ALIOTO, ROSEMARY ALIOTO, ALIOTO, FRANK ANGELO AS TRUSTEE OF THE ALIOTO, ALIOTO, ROSEMARY AS TRUSTEE OF THE ALIOTO FAMILY's Motion To Deem Facts Admitted And Strike The Answers Of Defendants Jill Kinney John Kinney And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Although the motion is unopposed, the relief sought is denied without prejudice. The defendants are in pro per and clearly have not followed statutory discovery rules or complied with the Court's previous Discovery Order dated October 29, 2013. However, they have made some attempt at informal compliance and therefore should be given one final opportunity to comply with the Court's previous Discovery Order. Failure to comply with this second opportunity will result in the striking of their Answers for willful non-compliance. Defendants shall within fifteen days of the date of service of this Order provide, without objection, verified discovery responses as was required under the previous Order of the Court dated October 29, 2013. The sanctions imposed in the October 29, 2013 Order in the sum of $500.00 remains due and owing. Defendants are further sanctioned in the sum of $760.00 applicable to the within motion. All sanctions owed to plaintiff shall be paid within 15 days of the date of service of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD WEISS VS. SHANNON WILLS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 27, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. (CCP § 473c.) Plaintiff's evidentiary objection nos. 1 and 2 are sustained. (As to Objection No. 2, the objection is sustained only because defendant did not provide a copy of the verification to the discovery responses in support of its motion.) Caltrans' objections nos. 7 and 8 are sustained, and objection nos. 1 - 6 are overruled. Caltrans' request for judicial notice is granted, except as to an earlier tentative ruling and order granting summary judgment as to Defendant City and County of San Francisco. Defendant Caltrans has met its initial burden that the location of the accident was not a dangerous condition. (Gov. Code §§ 830(a), 830.2, 835.) Defendant Wills did not exercise due care in a manner in which it is reasonably foreseeable that it will be used. (Sun v. City of Oakland (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1177, 1189.) It is undisputed that Defendant Wills crossed over the unbroken yellow line violating Veh. Code §§ 21460, 21650. (Defendant CalTrans UMF no. 7 and 9.) Defendant Wills testified that at the time the accident, the subject sign was the only sign that confused her and the only reason why she turned into the left lane. (UMF no. 10.) No similar accidents have occurred at the accident site since June 30, 2005. (See Callahan v. City and County of San Francisco (1971) 15 Cal.App.3d 374, 379; UMF no. 26.) The burden shifted to Plaintiff, who failed to establish a triable issue of fact to demonstrate that a dangerous condition existed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: UN PLAZA LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, LP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set No. One And Requests For Production, Set No. Two And Requests For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 27, 2013, Line 2 - PLAINTIFF UN PLAZA LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY, LIBERTY ASSET MANAGEMENT CORP., LUCY GAO and BENNY's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set No. One And Requests For Production, Set No. Two And Requests For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production applicable to discovery of limited partners identity, contribution or distributions, and related categories of inquiry, is denied. The very nature of a limited partner's relationship with an LLP is that there is no property interest in the specific partnership assets. The limited partner is, primarily, an investor, who contributes capital and thereby acquires the right to share in the business profits. The contribution must be in the form of cash or other property, may not consist of services and must be specified as to amount in the partnership certificate. The limited partner's surname may not be used as part of the firm name. The limited partner may not actively participate in the conduct of the business. Assuming the limited partner complies with these conditions, he/she is not liable as a general partner on business debts and obligations, except to the extent of his capital contribution. A limited partner's death or withdrawal will not dissolve the partnership and he/she is not a proper party to proceedings by or against the firm. "[T]he most striking feature of the relation of a special partner to the copartnership is its detached and impersonal character which accentuates sharply its dissimilarity from the relations of a general partner." Evans v. Galardi 128 Cal Rptr. 25 (1976. ) Plaintiff has not made a sufficient showing based on discussions occurring during mediation (which are inadmissible for any purpose per Evidence Code 1119) or based on the prosecution of a conversion cause of action (asserted solely against the general partnership) to overcome defendant's relevancy and privacy objections. Both parties' request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE S. WYNNS VS. BARCLAYS BANK DE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 27, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT BARCLAYS BANK DELAWARE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. The motion for summary judgment is timely filed and properly made, notwithstanding that Defendant filed a demurrer earlier in the case. Further, Defendant is not estopped from making the motion or the asserting the arguments in its. The Court considers Plaintiff's late submitted Amended Separate Statement filed December 16, 2013, although Plaintiff did not seek leave to file late additional papers. Plaintiff's objections to evidence are overruled in their entirety. As to the breach of contract cause of action, Defendant met its burden in providing evidence that shows that Plaintiff cannot establish each element of this cause of action. Plaintiff cannot establish that he performed under the Cardmember Agreement (the "Agreement") because the Agreement required monthly payments if there was a balance on the card. (Declaration of Peri Hutt, Ex. A; Defendant's Undisputed Fact ("UF") no. 5.) It is undisputed that Plaintiff stopped making these payments. (UF no. 21.) Plaintiff cannot establish that the agreement was breached because the evidence reveals there was no dispute to be resolved. Plaintiff agreed to a 17.99% interest rate, pursuant to the unambiguous terms of the Agreement. (Hutt Dec., Ex. A.) As to the declaratory relief cause of action, Defendant has met its burden in providing evidence that an accord and satisfaction did not occur. The unambiguous language of the Agreement states that Defendant "can accept late payments, partial payments, checks and money orders marked 'Paid in Full' or language having that same effect without losing any of [its] rights under this Agreement." (Hutt Dec., Ex. A; UF no. 5.) Further, the undisputed evidence establishes that the amount of the claim was not subject to a bona fide dispute, as required by Cal. Com. Code sec. 3311. (First Amended Complaint 9; UF nos. 2, 5..) As to the cause of action for violation of Civil Code section 1785.25, because Defendant met its burden of establishing that an accord and satisfaction did not occur, Defendant has also met its burden of establishing that it did not make an inaccurate or incomplete report to the credit reporting agencies. (UF nos. 19, 21, 22.) The burden then shifted to Plaintiff. Plaintiff has not met its burden of coming forward with evidence establishing a triable issue of material fact as to any of the causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS HUFFMAN VS. EBAY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Compliance With Court Order And For Monetary, Evidence/Issue And/Or Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 27, 2013, Line 4 - PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS HUFFMAN's Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And For Monetary, Evidence/Issue And Or Terminating Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is denied as to compliance with RFPD 7. The Court agrees with defendant the appropriate resolution for correction of an error in the Order dated October 22, 2013 was and is to request Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles to address that issue. As to the Special Interrogatory responses referenced in Judge Riles' Order and required to be answered, there is discussion of further responses in emails between the attorneys, but apparently no formal responses were served. Shortly after the email exchanges defendant retained new counsel. Defense counsel does not discuss further responses to the Special Interrogatories in the Opposition to the motion. It appears, however, that some earlier agreements were reached whereby the plaintiff permitted the defendant "to stand by its initial response". It is unclear if that agreement applied to all Special Interrogatories subject to the order. A hearing is required to sort out whether there has been compliance with the Special Interrogatory component of the Order, if it in fact it remains open. As an alternative to a hearing, the parties may meet and confer to determine the status of each inquiry. If it is agreed some of the Special Interrogatories require a response, the specifics shall be stated in a proposed Order. The defendant shall then have ten days for the date of service of said Order to comply. Each side has requested Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: GUAN, AI-HUA, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS VS. R&G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC. D/B/A R&G LOUNGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 27, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANTs R&G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC. D/B/A R&G LOUNGE & KINSON WONG's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiff's fifth cause of action adequately states a cause of action. (See Lu v. Hawaiian Gardens Casino, Inc. (2010) 50 Cal.4th 592, 604 (the fact that there is no private right of action under Labor Code § 351 "does not necessarily foreclose the availability of other remedies" including "a common law action for conversion").) Further, Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts that Defendants interfered with his ownership right to the tips and gratuities, and that the amount of money is capable of identification. (First Amended Complaint, 72, 73.) (See Software Design & Application, Ltd. v. Hoefer & Arnett, Inc. (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 472, 485.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: GUAN, AI-HUA, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS VS. R&G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC. D/B/A R&G LOUNGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 27, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANTs R&G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC. D/B/A R&G LOUNGE, KINSON WONG & ANGEL LIU's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. Plaintiff's eighth cause of action adequately states a cause of action. (See Lu v. Hawaiian Gardens Casino, Inc. (2010) 50 Cal.4th 592, 604 (the fact that there is no private right of action under Labor Code § 351 "does not necessarily foreclose the availability of other remedies".) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: CYPRESS GG2, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. SYSTEM ENERGY RESOURCES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 27, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT SYSTEM ENERGY RESOURCES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. The Court has jurisdiction to consider and resolve Plaintiff's challenges to the arbitration appraisal award. In addition to the Complaint, Plaintiff has filed and served a motion to vacate that was timely filed. The Complaint and motion meet the material substantive and procedural requirements and conditions of a petition to vacate under the California Arbitration Act, except for its title. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 27, 2013, Line 12, PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO, INC.'s Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Writ Of Attachment. Continued to January 8, 2014, to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f).
